DEFENDANT XANDER BICYCLE CORPORATION's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-31-2024 Continued To Oct-08-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Jun-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Brian Johnson, Esq. 415-926-7247 Seven HIlls for Plaintiff Keep America Safe and Beautiful
Bailee Pelham, Esq. (949) 760-1121 BUCHALTER for Defendant XANDER BICYCLE

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT XANDER BICYCLE CORPORATION's MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

Defendant's motion to transfer venue is denied.

The Proposition 65 complaint alleges defendant corporation sold in San Francisco bike racks that contained DEHP without required health hazard warnings. (Id. at 1:21-27; 3:27-4:4.)

Defendant moves under CCP 395(a), 395.5, 396b, 397(a) and 398, but its arguments are unavailing. As defendant concedes, a corporation may be sued in any county where, as here, "the obligation or liability arises." (Memo. 6:1-14; CCP 395.5.) As defendant also concedes, Prop 65 actions are subject to CCP 393, which also sets venue where, as here, any part of the cause arose. (Memo. 6:7:11; Dow AgroSciences LLC v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 1067, 1077.) Venue is determined based on the complaint's allegations. (Brown v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 37 Cal.3d 477, 482.)

Mr. Johnson to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)
